title: Tests 1
description: Tests in Rust are functions annotated with `#[test]`. They use assert macros to verify conditions.
instructions: Write a test that verifies the function works correctly.
hints: Use `assert!()` to check that a boolean expression is true.
For the second test: assert!(!is_even(5)); - note the `!` to negate.
explanation: Tests in Rust are functions marked with `#[test]` inside a module with `#[cfg(test)]`. The `assert!` macro panics if its argument is false, causing the test to fail. The `#[cfg(test)]` attribute ensures test code is only compiled when running tests, not in production builds.
